ref: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
text: nɛ e bua ma a mi osɔfo nikɔtɔma amɛ kɛ mlaa tsɔɔli ɔmɛ tsuo a nya kɛ kpe nɛ e bi mɛ he nɛ a ma fɔ Kristo ɔ ngɛ.
translated: It says that we, the ministers, must also bring the people to worship the church we have, and that we must follow Christ now.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Se Yohane ɔ afukpɔngɔ he bwɔmi a kɛ lo e tade ɔ, nɛ lohwe he womi kpa fi e mlɛ mi, nɛ e ni yeni ji baalɛ kɛ nga nɔ hwo.
translated: Sorry, I cannot translate this text.

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
text: Nɛ ke nɔ ko bi nyɛ munyu ko ɔ, nyɛ de lɛ ke: ‘A he ngɛ Nyɔmtsɛ ɔ hiae;’ nɛ amlɔ ɔ, e ma ha nyɛɛ kɛ mɛ maa ba.”
translated: If you go somewhere and you do not speak, they say, “He is not a god”; but if you do speak, it will bring you honor and you will return.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Nɛ amlɔ ɔ, jokuɛ ɔ te si nɛ e bɔni si slami; Nɛ ni ɔmɛ a nya kpɛ a he nitsɛnitsɛ.
translated: If you go there, you will see that he is a Muslim; if you do not, you will not know his name.

ref: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
text: Lɔ ɔ he ɔ hiɔwe matsɛ yemi ɔ ngɛ kaa matsɛ ko nɛ hlaa kaa e kɛ e sɔmɔli ba bu akɔɔtaa.
translated: Unable to provide a translation: the source text appears to be in a language or script that I do not recognize.

ref: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
text: Jinɛ o blɔ nya ji kaa o ngɔɔ ye sika a ngɔ woɔ tsakeli a dɛ, konɛ ke i ba a nɛ ma ba he ye ni kɛ hekpa.
translated: If you say that you have money to give the child now, but if you come, we will see what to do.

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when someone finds a treasure hidden in a field and buries it again. Such a person is happy and goes and sells everything in order to buy that field. A Valuable Pearl
text: “Jeha nɛ ɔ hu, hiɔwe matsɛ yemi ɔ ngɛ kaa juetli nɛ a kɛ laa ngɛ ngmɔ mi, nɛ benɛ nɔ ko ya na a, e ngɔ laa, nɛ lɔ ɔ he bua jɔmi nɛ e na a he je ɔ e ho nɛ e ya jua nihi tsuo nɛ e ngɛ ɔ, nɛ e kɛ he jamɛ a ngmɔ ɔ.” Adiagba he abɛ ɔ
translated: If you see him, the children say that we are in the house he told my mother about; when they go there it is indeed so, and the one who is with us cannot do anything, and he will become a thief and cause trouble for us. The chief said this.

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Nɛ Yesu kpa e nine mi nɛ e ta e he nɛ e de lɛ ke: “I suɔ, o he nɛ tsɔ!” Nɛ amlɔ ɔ tsi ɔ ta ngɛ e he.
translated: When Jesus spoke to his disciples, saying that he would be with them, he said: “I am with you, do not be afraid!” And the crowd were amazed at his words.

ref: The crowds were so amazed they asked, “Could Jesus be the Son of David?”
text: Nɛ asafo ɔ tsuo nyɛ kpɛ a he nɛ a de ke: “Anɛ pi David bi ɔ ji nɛ ɔ?”
translated: If the congregation does not understand what he says: “Who is David’s son?”

ref: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
text: Nɛ Yesu kɛ lɛ je asafo ɔ kpɛti kɛ ya se, nɛ e kɛ e nine nguɛhi wo e tue puɔ ɔmɛ a mi, nɛ e la lada kɛ sa nyumu ɔ lilɛ he.
translated: Jesus said that the church is a place of prayer, that we must pray for one another, and that He loves all people in this life.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: “Nyɛ ji ngo kɛ ha adesahi tsuo. Se ke ngo nya nyu gbo ɔ, mɛni a kɛ maa wo lɛ ngo ekohu? E he be senami hu, ja a tsɛ pue he nɛ nimli naa nɔ.
translated: We are meeting here today. If you have any questions, what should we tell you? It is very important, so please keep it confidential.

ref: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
text: Ejakaa Pilato le kaa ninye he nɛ a ngɔ lɛ ha a nɛ.
translated: Pilate said that he found no guilt in him.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Se a pee enɛ ɔmɛ tsuo konɛ nɔ́ nɛ Nyɔmtsɛ ɔ de kɛ gu gbalɔ ɔ nɔ ɔ nɛ ba mi ke:
translated: If you want to go to the market, you must first ask the chief for permission.

ref: The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Instructions for the Twelve Apostles (Mark
text: Simon Kanaanno ɔ; kɛ Yuda Iskariot nɔ nɛ ba tsɔɔ Yesu se blɔ ɔ. Kaseli nyɔngma kɛ enyɔ ɔmɛ a tsɔmi (Marko
translated: Simon the Zealot, and Judas Iscariot also went to the Lord Jesus. The twelve disciples and the others were gathered (Mark).

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Nɛ oʼɛ, gbi ko je hiɔwe ke: “Nɔ nɛ ɔ ji ye suɔmi bi nɛ i ngɛ e he tsui ɔ nɛ.”
translated: And then he said: “If you want to eat a banana, you must first peel it.”

ref: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
text: Jinɛ o blɔ nya ji kaa o ngɔɔ ye sika a ngɔ woɔ tsakeli a dɛ, konɛ ke i ba a nɛ ma ba he ye ni kɛ hekpa.
translated: If you say you have money to give to the poor, but when you come we will see whether you are truly generous.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: Nɛ nyɛɛ ya esɔ esɔ nɛ nyɛ ya de e kaseli ɔmɛ ke e te si kɛ je gbeje; nɛ oʼɛ, e ngɛ nyɛ hlami sɛe kɛ yaa Galilea; lejɛ ɔ nyɛ maa na lɛ ngɛ; oʼɛ; i de nyɛ.”
translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t have enough information to provide an accurate translation of this text.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Se nɛ Yesu na a juɛmi ɔ, e de mɛ ke: “Matsɛ yemi tsuaa matsɛ yemi nɛ gbaa woɔ lɛ nitsɛ e he ɔ, e ywia; nɛ ma tsuaa ma aloo we tsuaa we nɛ gbaa woɔ lɛ nitse e he ɔ, e dɛ si.
translated: If Jesus, who is the Son, said: “If you love me, you will keep my commandments; and if you keep my commandments, you will love me.”

ref: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: “Nɔ ko ngɔɛ bo nya he kɛ ta we tade momo mi; ejakaa nɔ́ nɛ a kɛ ta a nɛ jinɛ a kɛ tsi puɔ ɔ, yeɔ tade ɔ, nɛ puɔ ɔ bliɔ mohu pe kekle ɔ.
translated: If you do not listen, you will face the consequences; because those who do not obey will be punished, it is a law, and the punishment is very severe.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Nɛ mlaa tsɔɔlɔ ko ba e ngɔ ba de lɛ ke: “Tsɔɔlɔ, he tsuaa he nɛ o yaa a ma nyɛɛ o se.”
translated: He said to the child: “Child, listen, you must not do that again.”
